artifacts.summary: Firecrawl-first discovery and direct HTTP verification found public DTU-controlled AI guidance on ai.dtu.dk, student.dtu.dk, and bibliotek.dtu.dk. Approved claims are limited to directly supported rules, guidance, tool-availability, privacy/security selection, and academic-integrity statements. Official news and historical/minimal pages were rejected or not used as binding policy evidence.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a staged claim because it is directly supported by the DTU AI Info Hub Danish FAQ text. Wording preserves selected-course/current-exam limits.
Approved as source-scoped student-use guidance; no broader universal permission is inferred.
Approved with narrow teaching-materials wording matching the FAQ question and answer.
Approved as a current source-status/tool-treatment claim; wording avoids implying that every tool is allowed.
Approved with should-level wording from the Danish source; not escalated to a universal must beyond the quoted context.
Approved as guidance for activities where a responsible person would impose the tool on others at DTU; not generalized to personal use.
Approved as project-report/final-project guidance from DTU Library; not extended to every possible course artifact.
Approved because the Danish exam-cheating rules directly classify unreferenced AI-generated writing/task solving as plagiarism.

artifacts.title: Technical University of Denmark staged AI policy crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source crawl for Technical University of Denmark (QS 2026 rank 107) found DTU-controlled AI guidance covering generative AI in teaching and open-internet exams, citation and declaration expectations, tool-selection guidance, Copilot-for-GDPR/critical-content guidance, official-channel review for imposed tool use, and exam-cheating rules for unreferenced AI-generated work.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not marked human_reviewed, canonical, or publish-ready.
DTU's AI rules page is bilingual; Danish evidence snippets were preferred where available as original-language evidence.
Official DTU news pages were treated as background and were not used as binding policy evidence.
A historical student announcement page rendered without substantive accessible AI policy text and was rejected for low policy specificity.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
